description: Mexican drug cartel based in Matamoros, Tamaulipas, founded in the 1930s by Juan Nepomuceno Guerra and Juan García Ábrego. Originally involved in liquor smuggling during Prohibition, it later became one of Mexico's oldest drug trafficking organizations. The cartel has a significant presence in several Mexican states and has spawned numerous splinter groups, including Los Zetas. It is designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. State Department.

country_links.evidence_quote: García Ábrego logró asegurar el 50% del envío fuera de Colombia como pago por entrega... garantizar la llegada del producto desde Colombia a su destino.
country_links.context: The Gulf Cartel sourced cocaine from Colombia, negotiating shipments with the Cali Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territorio: Tamaulipas , Nuevo León , San Luis Potosí , Veracruz , Guatemala
country_links.context: The Gulf Cartel has territory in Guatemala.

country_links.evidence_quote: el contrabando de licores a Estados Unidos durante la época de la ley seca... Su base de operaciones se encuentra en Heroica Matamoros, Tamaulipas, México y que a su vez colinda con la ciudad de Brownsville, Texas, Estados Unidos.
country_links.context: The Gulf Cartel smuggles drugs and alcohol into the United States, has bases along the border, and launders money there.
